item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Either way, the format is unobtrusive and doesn't distract from the SCP itself.

Much like SCP-3189, this seems to draw on a wider mythos I'm unfamiliar with, but it's still interesting enough on its own to get a +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I use it in all my skips starting with 3622.

Unfortunately, due to staff duties, this is my first skip since 3622.

It can be important to the article, but isn't always. In 3622's case, it was. It's more giving me the option than being a hard and fast answer.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the article format here unique to the skip or your authorial lilt like djkaktus' headers? I haven't seen it before.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: As a 714 rewrite, this is good. The format is cool-looking, but it is clearly an online document. It's visually interesting in a way the foundation, in-universe, doesn't need. I'm indifferent. As a series 4 article, this falls flat. I upvoted, but only because I just read 714. If I came to this cold, I probably would have down-voted.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the format, but unfortunately there wasn't a single aspect here that grabbed my attention.

item.title: Author Post
item.content:encoded:
So I've been sitting on this a while, and figured if I don't post it, I never will.

It started off as a 714 rewrite, and ended up like this. I hope you guys enjoy.

Image is sourced from https://commons.m.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Thumb_Ring1.jpg and is CC compliant.

EDIT:

For everyone saying this is flat, or doesn't grab you, if you haven't at least read An Alchemist Goes to War (or any of the Alchemy tales series) then you're going to probably going to have zero basis here.

If you don't want to read any of that…then I don't know what to tell you. I can't force people to read stuff, but it's kind of a shame if you are unwilling to read a couple articles (or even one) to understand another one.
